milestone: MOE-CERT-3 (fold C4 -> L2; built on MOE-CERT-2 + MOE-CERT-2-ext)
headline: MoE-certified L2 (whole model)
headline_note: Per ADR-007 the bare word 'certified' is forbidden for MoE. This family is 'MoE-certified L2' = L1 + C4. L1: C1 (per-expert) exhaustive over ALL 24 layers x 60 routed experts = 1440 experts + C2 (router top-k set equality) on all 24 layers, both on the REAL trained weights vs a float64 reference computed one expert at a time; C3 (attention) via the existing mechanism cert. C4 (assembly/topology): the Qwen-MoE scale-topology end-to-end cert vs HF f64 = 1.490e-7. This is L2, NOT the dense ADR-004 CERTIFIED guarantee, and NOT L3/L4 — L3 (active-path C5) and L4 (global F64, unreachable) remain pending. L2 = 'the pieces (real weights, all layers) AND their composition (topology mechanism) are certified'.

C1_per_expert.scope: ALL 24 layers x 60 routed experts = 1440 experts (exhaustive, not sampled)
C1_per_expert.weights: real trained Qwen1.5-MoE-A2.7B weights, all layers (bf16 on disk; 7 layers span two shards)
C1_per_expert.oracle: NumPy float64, one expert at a time, one layer at a time (no full-model F64)
C1_per_expert.metric: max_abs_diff(Atenia expert forward, F64 reference) per expert
C1_per_expert.gate: max_abs_diff < 0.5 for EVERY expert (ADR-004 bar, unchanged)
C1_per_expert.result: all 1440 experts < 0.5 (global worst ~4.8e-7, ~1.0e6x inside the gate)
C1_per_expert.shared_expert_note: the always-on shared expert is exercised by the existing block/convention cert; C1 here covers the 60 routed experts per layer.

C2_router.hard_gate: top-k expert SET EQUALITY vs the F64 reference, every layer
C2_router.routing_margin_note: gap between the 4th and 5th router logit per layer (fragility signal, not a gate). The tightest layer (6, margin 0.0018) still matched the f64 top-k set exactly — no flip. Layer-0 margin 0.160333.
C2_router.result: top-k set identical on all 24 layers; tightest margin 0.0018 still matched

C3_attention.evidence: existing Qwen-MoE full-forward certificate — GQA + Q/K/V bias vs HF f64 = 5.960e-08 (MOE-FULL-13, docs/HANDOFF_MOE_FULL_13.md, tests/moe_certification_test.rs)
C3_attention.caveat: certifies the attention MECHANISM on a representative tiny fixture, NOT the real 14.3B attention weights re-certified per layer. Recorded, not over-claimed.

C4_assembly_topology.topology: qwen2_moe: 16 experts, top-4, shared expert (sigmoid gate), GQA 8/2-kv, Q/K/V bias, norm_topk_prob=false, 2 layers — the Qwen-MoE routing/attention topology at reduced hidden dim
C4_assembly_topology.metric: end-to-end full-forward max_abs_diff vs HuggingFace f64
C4_assembly_topology.gate: < 0.5 (ADR-004 bar, unchanged); the test asserts the stricter 1e-3 and also per-position argmax match + generate->EOS [10] + determinism
C4_assembly_topology.result: PASS (1.490e-7, ~3.4e6x inside the 0.5 gate; argmax exact; generate->EOS; deterministic)
C4_assembly_topology.caveat: certifies the routing+combine+assembly+attention MECHANISM end-to-end at full F64 on a REDUCED-DIM, 16-expert, RANDOM-weight topology fixture — NOT the real 60-expert trained weights. Per ADR-007 this is exactly the C4 evidence; it transfers to the real model only IN CONJUNCTION WITH C1/C2 (whole-model, real weights), which are met. Recorded, not over-claimed.

C5_active_path.blocker: real-weight active-path forward vs F64-active (~22 GB) or F32 cross-framework (~11 GB) not yet run (MOE-CERT-4).

pending_levels.blocker: C5 active-path — real-weight forward vs F64-active (~22 GB) or F32 cross-framework (~11 GB), not yet run (MOE-CERT-4).
global model.double() F64 ~114 GB — reserved / unreachable on the project hardware envelope; never fabricated.

remaining_caveats: C3 attention is reused at the mechanism level (tiny fixture, 5.960e-08), not re-certified on the real per-layer attention weights.

C4 assembly/topology is certified on a reduced-dim, 16-expert, random-weight topology fixture (qwen_scale, 1.490e-7) — the Qwen-MoE mechanism, NOT the real 60-expert trained-weight assembly. It is the C4 evidence ADR-007 designates; it gains real-weight force only combined with the whole-model C1/C2.

C1/C2 use a single deterministic probe input applied to every layer's real weights (a representative activation, not a real inter-layer activation trace) — this certifies the per-layer expert/router math, consistent with the layer-0 approach.
L2 is NOT L3 (active-path on the real model as run) and NOT L4 (global F64, unreachable), and is NOT the dense ADR-004 CERTIFIED.

verification.reference_all_layers: fixtures/moe/qwen_moe_decomp_ref_all_layers.safetensors (+ .json) — MOE-CERT-2-ext all-24-layer reference, float64 one expert at a time (~11.8 MB)
verification.expected: C1 global worst max_abs_diff ~4.8e-7 (< 0.5, all 1440 experts, layer 6 / expert 37); C2 top-k set match on all 24 layers; min routing margin 0.001834 (layer 6); 0 failures.
verification.ci_smoke: cert2_harness_mechanics_smoke (non-ignored) exercises the harness on the committed tiny fixture without the 27 GB model.

generated_on: F64 reference: PyTorch (bf16->float64) + NumPy float64, Windows 11. Atenia: CPU forward, RTX 4070 Laptop host, 32 GB RAM.
